findings.title: /api/sync bulk trip_days loop has NO authorization gate — cross-tenant day write/inject (IDOR sibling of the R2 /api/days fix)
findings.rootCause: The `/api/sync` trip_days loop (data.py:793-838) writes `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE` keyed only on `WHERE trip_days.deleted_at IS NULL`. It trusts `d['tripId']` verbatim and has NO membership check. The sibling loops in the SAME handler ARE gated: the trips loop does `if existing and t['id'] not in editable_trip_ids: continue` (data.py:302-306) and the expenses loop gates on `expense_writable_trip_ids`. The per-row POST /api/days route was hardened against exactly this (R2 fix, days.py:77-91, with test test_days_single_row_upsert_blocks_cross_trip_hijack), but the bulk-path sibling was missed. The loop also omits the dayNumber validation (negative/huge/fractional) that days.py:50-74 enforces. The first-party client now sends `{}` to /api/sync (api.ts:80), so there is no first-party trigger, but the route still accepts and writes the payload from any caller — and AI-accepted days use predictable ids `'day_'+Date.now()+'_'+idx` (useAiPlan.ts:466), making targeted overwrites feasible even without an id leak.

findings.title: Deleting a day silently RESURRECTS its pruned photos/documents — server delete_day media cascade undone by the client's loss-free union merge
findings.rootCause: Frontend deleteDay (handlers.ts:272-325) removes the day from STATE.tripDays and calls deleteDayOnServer, but never cleans the local `trip.photos`/`trip.documents`/`trip.markedPlaces` of items tagged with the deleted dayId, and never calls fetchTripMedia. delete_day bumps trips.media_updated_at when it prunes (days.py:338-347), so the client's next persistTripMedia gets a 409. The 409 handler (media.ts:128-159) merges via `_mergeMediaField`, which is a pure UNION by key (media.ts: keeps every server item + adds any LOCAL item missing from the server set). Because the server PRUNED the day-tagged items, they are 'missing' on the server side, so the union RE-ADDS the stale local copies and POSTs them back. The merge has zero deletion/tombstone awareness — the comment at media.ts:163-164 even claims 're-parking ... can't resurrect a peer's delete', which is exactly the wrong assumption: a server-side delete (cascade OR a peer removing a photo) is indistinguishable from 'an item the local side should re-add'.

findings.title: Parallel renumber upserts collide on UNIQUE(trip_id, day_number) after deleting a middle day → numbering gap + spurious 'another device updated this day' toast
findings.rootCause: deleteDay renumbers survivors in memory to a contiguous 1..N (handlers.ts:296-301) then persists them with `Promise.all(...map(upsertDay))` (handlers.ts:318-320) — UNORDERED parallel writes. The same pattern is in the edit-dates finalize (trip.ts:621). When numbers shift DOWNWARD into still-occupied slots, two parallel upserts race against the partial UNIQUE index idx_trip_days_trip_day_number(trip_id, day_number) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL. Example: survivors [d3→2, d4→3, d5→4]; if upsertDay(d5→4) executes before upsertDay(d4→3) commits, d5's target 4 still collides with d4's live row (number 4) → IntegrityError → days.py:161-181 returns 409. The client's 409 handler (api.ts:453-462) shows the stale-edit toast + pullFromServer but does NOT retry with a corrected number, so the survivor keeps its old number and a gap persists until reload.

findings.title: Read-only day modal (dayViewModal) is only half-internationalised — plan slot labels, notes labels and empty-states are hardcoded English
findings.rootCause: The dayView i18n block (en.ts:1571-1584) was added for this exact file ('Pre-fix every label ... was hardcoded English') but only covers the photos/documents/expert-tip section. The morning/afternoon/evening/notes/view-only/nothing-planned strings were left as literal English in the template (dayViewModal.ts), so t() is never called for them and they don't switch locale.

findings.title: Journaling modal is entirely hardcoded English (no i18n at all)
findings.rootCause: journalingModal.ts has no `import { t }` and uses raw English literals throughout the template literal and the save toast (lines 31-45). It was missed in the i18n migration that internationalised the sibling dayDetailModal.

findings.title: Day-pin flow alerts are hardcoded English
findings.rootCause: These three showLiquidAlert calls in handlers.ts pass raw English strings; no matching i18n keys exist (grep for the strings in locales/en.ts returns nothing). The surrounding code already imports t() and uses it for the delete-confirm and the Trip-Hub-cannot-delete error, so these were simply overlooked.

findings.title: Read-only day modal shows a blue 'Day 0' badge for the Trip Hub anchor instead of a Trip Hub chip
findings.rootCause: dayViewModal.ts only special-cases the anchor (Number(day.dayNumber)===0) for the photos/documents section labels (lines 104,110,113,118); the header badge unconditionally renders `Day ${day.dayNumber}` (line 68) and the title falls back to `Day ${dayNumber}` (line 72). The anchor-vs-numbered branching that dayDetailModal has (isAnchor, gold chip, Trip Hub title) was never ported to the read-only modal.

findings.title: Shortlist 'Add to AM/PM/Eve' ✓ marker and toggle use naive case-insensitive substring matching — false positives for short/substring place names corrupt the wrong plan line
findings.rootCause: Presence is detected with `planVals[time].includes(place.name.toLowerCase())` (line 744) and removal does `lines.findIndex(l => l.toLowerCase().includes(needle))` + splice (lines 862-863). Plain substring matching with no word-boundary or whole-line check; the add format is '- {name}' but the detector matches the bare name anywhere.

findings.title: Dropping a day pin on the map doesn't persist to local storage until 'Save pin' — navigating away loses the placed pin
findings.rootCause: The armed map-click listener in addDayPin sets day.lat/lon/lng and calls navigate('home', null, true) but does NOT emit('state:changed') (handlers.ts:113-119), so localStorage isn't updated and the server isn't written. Persistence happens only in saveDayPin (which emits + upsertDay). The in-memory mutation survives only until the next full reload/state hydration, after which it's gone. (cancelPinEdit and deleteDayPin both emit; the drop path is the inconsistent one.)
